untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a roll management strategy based on RAP (Roll Adjustment Period) calendar timing and volatility profile optimization. It requires: (1) detection of RAP period start/end dates (not available in price/technical indicators), (2) identification of 'early RAP days' vs 'late RAP days' (calendar/contract-specific logic), and (3) volatility profile comparison across RAP days (would require historical volatility ranking within RAP, not a standard indicator). The signal is a portfolio/contract management rule, not a price-based entry/exit condition. Standard OHLCV and technical indicators cannot operationalize 'early vs late RAP day' timing without explicit date/calendar data and contract metadata.
translation_notes: This is a roll scheduling optimization strategy for futures contracts, not a traditional trading signal. It requires contract calendar knowledge (RAP dates), day-of-RAP sequencing, and cross-day volatility profiling — all outside the scope of price-based technical indicator rules.
